title: What is the difference between the findBy and query method keywords provided by Spring Data JPA?

answers.junior: findBy lets you create queries by naming methods after entity fields. @Query lets you write custom queries using JPQL or SQL.
answers.middle: findBy is used for simple property-based lookups, automatically generating queries. @Query allows custom JPQL or SQL for complex queries. Use findBy for maintainability, @Query for flexibility.
answers.senior: findBy leverages query derivation for concise, type-safe queries. @Query provides full control for advanced logic, joins, or projections. Prefer findBy for simple queries, switch to @Query for complex requirements.
answers.tricky: Overusing findBy for complex logic can reduce clarity and performance. Always validate custom queries for correctness and efficiency. Use native SQL in @Query only when necessary, and monitor query performance to avoid N+1 issues.
